memories.memory_text: I remember the cool weight of the new breathing apparatus fitting snugly against my neck, the sleek design a quiet sign that things were changing—then seeing Hellboy’s photo, grinning mid-leap, and murmuring, 'Oh, Lord—he's smiling in this one.'
grounded_facts: Abe receives updated equipment, including a sleeker breathing apparatus and new goggles
Abe looks at a photo of Hellboy in action, reacting to his expression
The equipment upgrade is visually noticeable and personally observed by Abe

memories.memory_text: I said Hellboy wasn't angry at Manning—just enthusiastic—when the wall suddenly exploded, fire tearing open the door, and I ducked behind the pillar, my voice trailing off as I saw him on the floor, smoking, saying 'Oh, hi Blue.'
grounded_facts: Abe defends Hellboy's behavior to Manning just before an explosion occurs
The concrete wall explodes, followed by a fireball swinging the door open
Hellboy is revealed lying on the floor, his back smoking, extinguishing flames
Abe physically reacts by cowering behind a pillar with Manning

memories.memory_text: I heard a high-pitched chitter-chatter no one else seemed to notice, and when I touched the empty cage with the royal seal, I knew something was very wrong—creatures had escaped, and I could sense them nearby.
grounded_facts: Abe detects auditory stimuli (high-pitched chitter-chatter) that others do not react to.
Abe discovers an empty cage with the embossed royal seal on the iron door.
Abe uses his webbed fingers to interact with the environment, indicating tactile investigation.
Abe verbally reports detecting activity before identifying the presence of 'creatures.'

memories.memory_text: I found a human femur—wet and gnawed—right there on the floor. I remember the slick texture, the marks left by sharp teeth. I whispered, 'Ah, I knew it,' because the evidence confirmed what I'd feared.
grounded_facts: Abe finds a human femur that is wet and gnawed.
Abe says, 'Ah, I knew it,' immediately after finding the femur.

memories.memory_text: I noticed the floor wasn't a rug—Red lifted his foot and saw it was thick with excrement. Then it clicked: they digest instantly, excrete quickly, and consume everything. No remains. No corpses. Just this... layer of waste.
grounded_facts: Abe observes the floor rippling and realizes it is not a rug but a thick layer of excrement.
Abe deduces the creatures have a very active metabolism, excrete immediately after eating, and leave no corpses because there are no leftovers.

memories.memory_text: Through my goggles, the room shifted—boxes became a tree trunk, iron beams turned to stone columns, and there, where a banner had hung, a massive rusted lock appeared. I said, 'There—' and knew it was the mechanism blocking our way.
grounded_facts: Abe uses his specialized goggles to see through an illusion in the basement.
The visual transformation reveals a hidden locking mechanism where a banner previously appeared to be.
Abe verbally identifies the object as a 'locking mechanism' immediately after perceiving it.

memories.memory_text: I saw her slippered feet first—pearls and gold embroidery glinting in the filth, so out of place among the mud and vermin. That small detail pulled me forward, made me wonder who she could be.
grounded_facts: Abe sees the woman's slippered feet adorned with pearls and gold embroidery while she moves through the crowd.
The feet are visually striking against the dirty, grim environment of the Troll Market.

memories.memory_text: When her cowl fell back, I recognized her—her face, her presence, like light in that wretched place. And then I saw the bracelet on her bandaged arm, the seal's coat of arms unmistakable. I was thunderstruck.
grounded_facts: Abe sees the Princess's face when her cowl swings aside at a market stall.
He observes the royal bracelet with the seal's coat of arms on her bandaged arm.
He experiences a profound emotional reaction, described as being thunderstruck.

memories.memory_text: I woke up in that dusty map shop, disoriented, and the first thing I noticed was my belt—my weapons—gone. When she told me her guards had taken them, I felt exposed, vulnerable in a way I hadn’t in years.
grounded_facts: Abe wakes up in a room filled with ancient scrolls and books
He realizes his belt and weapons are missing
The Princess informs him her guards took his weapons
He feels shame and vulnerability during the confrontation

memories.memory_text: She asked me to give her my hand, and when her fingers traced my palm, something stirred—warmth, recognition. Later, even with Red bursting in, I insisted she was no threat, that we should honor her request.
grounded_facts: The Princess asks Abe for his hand
She touches his palm, smiles, and declares him a friend
Abe is visibly affected, emotionally moved
He tells Hellboy to stop, vouches for the Princess, and insists on showing her respect

memories.memory_text: I remember moving like water—ducking and weaving, feeling the rhythm of each strike coming, parrying just enough to redirect the force. It was as if my body remembered the form before my mind could catch up.
grounded_facts: Abe demonstrates the way of the water, a fluid martial arts display, while shielding the Princess.
He ducks and parries blows, moving with agility and precision.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed two bowling balls and rolled them hard at the guards—I heard the impact as they staggered back. When they rose, I snatched up the pins nearby and swung them like clubs, using what the alley gave me.
grounded_facts: Abe scoops up bowling balls and rolls them at the Prince's guards, knocking a couple off their feet.
He uses bowling pins as improvised clubs after the guards begin to rise.

memories.memory_text: I remember the way her voice softened as she read Bronte’s words about a string tied beneath the ribs—something in me recognized that feeling, even before she closed the book and I confessed how I’d never known Charlotte, though I wished I had.
grounded_facts: The Princess was reading a passage from 'Jane Eyre' about an emotional connection symbolized by a string under the ribs.
Abe expressed admiration for Charlotte Bronte, noting she died shortly before he was born and that it would have been a privilege to know her.

memories.memory_text: When she spoke of sensing her twin brother across distance, of a bond from childhood in Renvyle, I hesitated—but then I believed her, because my own instinct, not logic, told me she was speaking a truth I could not measure.
grounded_facts: The Princess claimed a psychic connection with her twin brother, rooted in their shared past in Renvyle, Ireland.
Abe acknowledged relying on intuition when he said he trusted her not by reason but by feeling, stating he'd never met anyone like her.

memories.memory_text: I told Red I think I'm in love—with the Princess—because she's like me, a creature from another world, and for the first time, I didn't feel alone. I could barely get the words out, stammering like I always do when something matters.
grounded_facts: Abe confesses to Hellboy that he thinks he's in love with the Princess.
Abe describes feeling alone his whole life until seeing the Princess, who he believes is like him.
Abe uses hesitant, emotional speech while revealing this to Hellboy.

memories.memory_text: After talking about Father, I sipped beer and said Manilow suddenly made sense to me—then Red and I started singing 'Can't Smile Without You' together, quietly at first, then loud, almost crying. I don't know when I last sang with someone like that.
grounded_facts: Abe expresses missing Father and wishes he could give guidance.
Abe comments on newly appreciating Barry Manilow's music in a moment of emotional openness.
Abe and Hellboy sing 'Can't Smile Without You' together, becoming teary-eyed.

memories.memory_text: I opened the hood of the Hummer and said it was an unfortunate vehicle. The waste of precious fossil fuels bothered me.
grounded_facts: Abe opened the hood of his Hummer.
Abe called the Hummer an unfortunate vehicle.
Abe expressed concern about the waste of fossil fuels.

memories.memory_text: I stood beside Hellboy and Liz as the applause rose, feeling the weight of so many eyes on us—this was the first time I was introduced by name to the world, not just a hidden figure in the shadows.
grounded_facts: Abe Sapien is publicly named alongside Hellboy, Liz Sherman, and Agent Manning on a television show.
The introduction is met with uproarious applause.
The event occurs while Johann and other agents watch the broadcast from the garbage truck lab.

memories.memory_text: I remember the bright lights of the camera turning to me, and how Mr. Leno’s joke about my neck collar made the audience laugh—I chose to speak calmly, explaining my biology as clearly as I could, though my voice trembled slightly with restraint.
grounded_facts: Abe Sapien is on live television and is addressed by Jay Leno.
Jay Leno makes a joke about Abe's neck apparatus, referring to it as a 'toilet seat'.
Abe responds by explaining that his lungs function like gills and require nitrogen-to-oxygen conversion to prevent suffocation within five minutes.
The camera focuses on Abe during his response.

memories.memory_text: I remember the blare of the alarms as we rushed from the Hummer into the garage, the cold concrete beneath my feet, and the sight of fallen agents on the dock—something was very wrong, and we had to move fast.
grounded_facts: Abe is present in the BPRD garage during an active crisis.
He moves with Hellboy, Liz, and Johann into the building.
He observes dead agents on the loading dock.
Alarms are ringing loudly in the scene.

memories.memory_text: I saw her—arms bound, held by the hair—and before I could stop myself, I ran toward her. The troll’s claws sliced the air just past my chest, but I didn’t care. She was in pain, and I had to reach her.
grounded_facts: Abe sees the Princess with her arms tied and being held by the hair by her brother.
Abe shouts 'Princess!' and impulsively moves forward without thinking.
A troll swipes at Abe, the claws narrowly missing his chest.
Hellboy restrains Abe after he attempts to rush forward again.

memories.memory_text: I saw her head snap back, heard her moan—and I cried out, 'H.B., no! Stop—you mustn’t harm him!' Only later did I realize: the blow wasn’t meant for her. I mistook her pain for his doing.
grounded_facts: During the fight, the Princess is in a troll's arms and her head moves violently as if struck.
A moan of pain escapes the Princess.
Abe shouts 'H.B., no! Stop—you mustn’t harm him!' believing Hellboy is causing her pain.
Abe does not realize the Prince is still fighting and that the movement is due to the struggle.

memories.memory_text: I touched the spear with my webbed fingers, and I felt it move—alive, wriggling deeper toward Red’s heart. I had to explain, even though Liz didn’t want to see it.
grounded_facts: Abe physically touched the magical spear protruding from Hellboy's sternum.
The spear reacted to Abe's touch by moving deeper into Hellboy's body.
Abe attempted to show Liz the spear's movement, but she refused to look.
Abe identified the spear as a magical alloy that cannot be safely removed by him.

memories.memory_text: I found the gold piece hidden in Chapter 23 of *Jane Eyre*, just where I’d left it, but when Liz came in, I closed my hand around it without thinking—then lied to her face.
grounded_facts: Abe retrieves the gold piece from his copy of *Jane Eyre*, specifically at Chapter 23.
Liz enters the library and asks if he found anything.
Abe hides the gold piece in his palm and tells Liz 'No. Sorry.'
He later conceals the gold piece by tucking it into his belt.
Abe appears deeply troubled after lying to Liz.

memories.memory_text: I moistened the cylinder's surface and pressed it onto the map—suddenly the hidden glyphs appeared, and I saw the name: Renvyle. That was the place, where both royal siblings were born, and where they held something sacred.
grounded_facts: Abe used the cylinder case to reveal hidden markings on the map by moistening it and stamping it onto paper.
The revealed markings included glyphs, coordinates, and the silhouette of a twisted tree.
Abe identified Renvyle as the birthplace of the Princess and her brother.

memories.memory_text: I stood there quietly at first, watching Liz come in, her eyes full of tears, and then I found my voice—soft but sure—saying we’d make it alright, that Manning didn’t matter, that it was just the three of us now.
grounded_facts: Abe was present in Hellboy's room, standing nearby in silence before speaking.
Abe addressed Liz directly, affirming her resolve and rejecting Manning's authority.
Abe explicitly stated loyalty to the trio: 'We should just do this together: the three of us.'

memories.memory_text: I couldn't remember the procedure, not even the simplest controls—just flipping through the manual, grasping at terms like 'port' and 'NA-7488-BD,' but it was no use. I had to admit it: I can't pilot this thing.
grounded_facts: Abe is in the pilot's seat reading an airplane manual.
He attempts to recall or apply information from the manual, including a mnemonic about 'port' meaning 'left.'
He expresses despair and admits he cannot pilot the plane.

memories.memory_text: I raised my webbed hand to wave from the cockpit as the plane spun in circles, exhaust fumes filling the hangar, knowing there was no turning back.
grounded_facts: Abe is inside the BPRD airplane in the hangar.
The plane is moving erratically, knocking into objects.
Abe sees agents in the doorway and Manning arriving.
He visibly waves goodbye with his webbed hand.
The plane taxis onto the runway after the maneuver.

memories.memory_text: I inserted the crystal cylinder into the slot in the tree and twisted it—just as the map suggested—and for a moment, I wasn't sure if anything would happen. Then the roots began to move, rising from the earth like waking serpents.
grounded_facts: Abe inserts and twists the crystal cylinder into a slot in the tree.
The tree's roots lift from the ground and form an archway after the cylinder is activated.
Abe had previously compared the tree to a drawing on the unfurled map.
Liz urged Abe to try inserting the cylinder.
Hellboy and Johann expressed caution before the activation.

memories.memory_text: Through the binoculars, I saw it—a second cave, half-hidden behind the crashing waves along the coast. I noted its position, distinct from the Angel of Death's cave, though I couldn't yet grasp what lay inside.
grounded_facts: Abe is alone on a hilltop surveying the coastline.
Abe uses binoculars to observe a second cave.
The second cave is partially concealed by waves.
Abe sees the Angel of Death's cave in the distance.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing before the twisted tree, my voice barely above a whisper as I said, 'Do it, please. I - I'm ready for the real world now.'
grounded_facts: Abe Sapien is present at the twisted tree with Johann.
Johann holds the map cylinder and prepares to activate the mechanism.
Abe speaks quietly, expressing his readiness to enter the real world.
The activation of the cylinder causes the tree roots to form an arch.
The BPRD team passes through the arch after the activation.

memories.memory_text: I stepped forward, removed my gunbelt and equipment pack, and handed them to Mr. Manning, telling him I quit—I could see the shock on his face, but I had to leave the BPRD behind.
grounded_facts: Abe removes his gunbelt and equipment pack in front of Manning and other agents.
Abe addresses Manning formally and expresses gratitude before stating 'I quit.'
Abe walks away after surrendering his equipment, marking his resignation from the BPRD.

memories.memory_text: I held the door for Liz, even though she wouldn’t look at me. Just kept it open, like a damn gentleman.
grounded_facts: Hellboy holds the door open for Liz as they enter the garbage truck in the garage weapons area.
The gesture occurs amid tension, with Liz being tight-lipped and avoiding interaction.

memories.memory_text: I heard scratching, then Abe's voice crackling over the intercom—more than one, he said, maybe a hundred or two. The wall bulged right in front of me and split open, ceiling cracking too, so I loaded my shotgun fast.
grounded_facts: Hellboy hears a scratching noise in the corridor.
Abe speaks over the intercom, indicating a large number of approaching threats.
Hellboy reacts to the wall and ceiling breaking open.
Hellboy begins loading a lever-action shotgun in response.

memories.memory_text: I yanked the pins on the whole belt of Vulcan grenades with those damn tooth fairies crawling all over me—felt the charge build right before the world went white.
grounded_facts: Hellboy retrieves a belt of Vulcan grenades from his backpack
He holds the grenade belt aloft and declares
Vulcan 66
boys! Come and get 'em-
He allows the creatures to swarm onto him before pulling all the pins

memories.memory_text: I exploded through the window in a fireball, still covered in those shrieking things, and came down hard on the street—felt the pavement crack under me.
grounded_facts: Hellboy is launched through an upper-floor window in a massive explosion
He is seen as a ball of flame arcing downward
He lands on the pavement below
cracking it
while still surrounded by writhing creatures

memories.memory_text: I shot those fairies one by one, then held up the last squirming one by its tail—flashes popping all around me, Manning looking like he wanted to vanish. I just shrugged and said, 'I guess we're out now.'
grounded_facts: Hellboy shot multiple fairies attacking a crowd
He physically removed and held up one fairy by the tail
He spoke directly to Manning in the presence of media
He was photographed by multiple cameras immediately after the action
He delivered the line 'I guess we're out now' with a shrug

memories.memory_text: Manning told me Washington wants a new public face for the BPRD, and it’s not me—some guy named Johann Kraus is taking over, a big shot from the European branch.
grounded_facts: Manning informs Hellboy that Washington wants a new public face for the BPRD.
Manning introduces Johann Kraus as the replacement, describing him as head of the European branch and a doctor from the University of Munich.
Hellboy interrupts Manning, expressing discomfort upon hearing the name 'Kraus' and stating he has a problem with Germans.

memories.memory_text: I saw Kraus in person—his head’s a glass bubble full of gas, and when he bowed to Liz with that smooth voice, all I could think was, 'That’s an open face for you...'
grounded_facts: Johann Kraus appears in a containment suit with a translucent glass head filled with gas.
Kraus approaches Liz, takes her hand, and bows while introducing himself in a velvety, otherworldly voice.
Hellboy whispers sarcastically to Manning, 'That's an open face for you...'

memories.memory_text: I remember asking Johann, 'What—? What—? A goblin army—?' when the Princess said the seal was a declaration of war, and seeing him sit down like the world just dropped out from under him.
grounded_facts: Hellboy retrieved the seal from the human side two nights prior.
The Princess identifies the seal as a declaration of war that awakens the Golden Army.
Hellboy reacts with confusion and alarm, asking Johann about a goblin army.
Johann reacts by sitting down heavily and expressing fear about the Golden Army.

memories.memory_text: I remember asking where the crown was, and the Princess saying her father had it destroyed—split into three pieces, one for mankind, two for the sons of the Earth. She held up her piece on a chain like it meant something.
grounded_facts: Hellboy asked 'Where is that crown??'
The Princess responded that her father had the crown destroyed and cut into three pieces
One piece was for mankind, two for the sons of the Earth
The Princess displayed her piece on a chain around her neck

memories.memory_text: She opened that cylinder and showed a map with just three spots—Tokyo, Moscow, Renvyle—no other details. Then she rolled it back up like it was nothing, but I knew it wasn't.
grounded_facts: The Princess opened the cylinder and revealed a parchment with three coordinates
The coordinates were Edo (Tokyo), Moscow, and Renvyle
The map showed only the three locations and nothing else
The Princess rolled the map back into the cylinder

memories.memory_text: I cracked my knuckles, felt the pop in my fingers, and started up the stairs—those guards weren't going to stop me from getting to him.
grounded_facts: Hellboy cracks his knuckles in the narrow passage of the Troll Market
He begins advancing up the stairs toward the Prince
Guards move to block his path

memories.memory_text: I cracked Johann over the head with my stone fist, and his glass head just shattered—didn't expect that. Then his suit collapsed, hissing like a punctured tank.
grounded_facts: Hellboy strikes Johann with his stone fist
Johann's glass head shatters upon impact
Johann's suit deflates with a hissing sound

memories.memory_text: The lockers came alive, slamming into my head over and over—couldn't even see straight. Johann's voice echoed, saying 'Speed is the key,' while his misty form drifted away.
grounded_facts: Lockers attack Hellboy by repeatedly striking his head
Hellboy is disoriented and imprinted on the locker metal
Johann's voice speaks from the lockers before his form exits

memories.memory_text: I told Johann to back off when he tried to tell me how to handle things with Liz—like I need his advice on what to say or who I am.
grounded_facts: Hellboy confronts Johann after seeing him talk to Liz
Johann gives unsolicited advice about not revealing a relationship with Liz
Hellboy responds with 'Thanks a bunch. I'll decide that.'

memories.memory_text: He said he knew my father—and that my father wouldn't have approved of me. I didn't know what to say, just stood there.
grounded_facts: Johann claims he knew Hellboy's father and considered him a friend
Johann states that Hellboy's father would not have approved of his behavior
Hellboy reacts silently, visibly taken aback

memories.memory_text: I was just sitting there when Leno asked if the dragon was my friend—like I’m some kind of monster sympathizer. Then he went after me, questioning why I wasn’t out 'protecting humanity' while hitting on American women. That’s when I snapped and tore the desk up, hurling it through the set.
grounded_facts: Hellboy was present on a live television broadcast in Moscow.
A dragon attacked during the broadcast, causing the feed to cut out.
Jay Leno asked Hellboy if the dragon was a friend of his.
Leno questioned Hellboy's loyalty and motives, implying he was not fulfilling his role.
Hellboy reacted violently by destroying the desk and throwing it through the backdrop.

memories.memory_text: I walked right up to the Prince and told him to start with me—then showed him my fist, calling it Five-fingered Mary. He wanted a fight, so I gave him one.
grounded_facts: Hellboy approaches the Prince directly during the standoff.
Hellboy says 'No. Why don't you just start with me?'
Hellboy raises his massive hand and names it 'Five-fingered Mary' in response to the Prince's question about his weapon.

memories.memory_text: I punched him hard, knocked him down, but when Abe shouted, I turned—then felt the spear go into my chest. The pain hit like a truck, and the last thing I saw was Liz and Johann rushing toward me.
grounded_facts: Hellboy lands a punch that knocks the Prince to his knees and follows with a combination.
Hellboy is distracted by Abe's shout and turns to look.
The Prince impales Hellboy in the chest with the spear.
Hellboy grimaces, looks down at the spear in his chest, and collapses.
Liz screams and, along with Johann, catches Hellboy as he falls.

memories.memory_text: I'm lying on the table, barely holding on—every time Abe touches the spear, it twists deeper, like it's alive, and I can feel it crawling toward my heart.
grounded_facts: Hellboy is on an operating table, barely conscious.
The magical spear in his sternum moves closer to his heart when touched.
He groans in pain when the spear reacts.

memories.memory_text: I'm leaning in the doorway, barely holding myself up, and the cats are all around me, purring like nothing's wrong—like they don't even notice I'm falling apart.
grounded_facts: Hellboy leans weakly against the doorway to his room
His cats are nearby, purring
He is in visible pain, though the cats are oblivious

memories.memory_text: Abe stands there quiet at first, then says Manning's orders be damned—that we stick together, the three of us. I didn't expect that. Not from him.
grounded_facts: Abe rejects Manning's orders
Abe declares loyalty to Hellboy and Liz, insisting they stay together as a trio
Hellboy is present and hears the declaration

memories.memory_text: I climbed up into the plane and saw Liz smiling at me—just that look, no words, but it hit me like a gut punch after everything that went down.
grounded_facts: Hellboy enters the BPRD airplane through the passenger compartment.
Liz is present inside the plane and smiles at Hellboy as he boards.
The scene takes place at night inside a hangar.

memories.memory_text: I remember Johann stepping in when Abe couldn't get the plane going, saying 'I'll give it a try' just before the engines fired up.
grounded_facts: Johann entered the cockpit and offered to help pilot the plane.
Abe admitted he couldn't pilot the plane and was in despair.
Johann sat in the co-pilot seat and connected with the controls.
The engines started after Johann interfaced with the plane.

memories.memory_text: I woke up in Liz's arms on the plane, sunlight hitting my face, and all I could manage was to ask where we were—my voice sounded weak, even to me.
grounded_facts: Hellboy is on a BPRD plane descending over an Irish island
He is in Liz's arms and appears physically weakened
He speaks to Liz with a weak, groaning voice
Early sunlight is visible in the cockpit
He asks Liz about their location

memories.memory_text: I heard that voice—cold, ancient—call me Anung-un-Rama, like it was a name I was supposed to know. Then it said I was meant to rule over the destruction of the Earth, and Liz shouted yes, she still wanted me to live.
grounded_facts: The Angel of Death addresses Hellboy as
Anung-un-Rama

and states his destiny to rule over the destruction of the Earth. Hellboy is unconscious during part of the exchange but regains awareness after the spear is removed. Liz confirms she wants Hellboy to live despite the warning. Hellboy hears the dialogue about his destiny upon regaining consciousness or during partial awareness.

memories.memory_text: I felt the tongs pull the spear out of my chest—white-hot pain—and then I saw her, the Angel of Death, holding up the bloody tip. I opened my eyes, weak, and gave her a faint smile.
grounded_facts: The Angel of Death uses red-hot tongs to remove the spear embedded in Hellboy's chest. Hellboy cries out in pain during the removal. After the spear is removed
Hellboy's eyes flutter open and he looks at the Angel of Death. Hellboy smiles weakly at the Angel of Death after waking.

memories.memory_text: I looked at Liz, really looked, and asked her—'Am I a good man, Liz?? Am I??'—because if she didn’t think so, I wasn’t sure I could keep going.
grounded_facts: Hellboy is bandaged and sitting outside the hut
recovering physically. He asks Liz twice if he is a good man
showing emotional urgency. He ties his moral worth directly to her opinion
saying he doesn’t care about the world as long as she stays with him. The dialogue occurs in a quiet moment before Johann interrupts. The scene includes a beat of silence after his question
indicating emotional weight.

memories.memory_text: When Johann showed up and told me to stay behind, I called him 'GLASSHOLE' and said I'd be damned before I let him keep me from watching over Liz.
grounded_facts: Johann returns and suggests Hellboy stay behind to rest. Hellboy responds sarcastically and defiantly
rejecting the idea. He uses the phrase 'Like Hell I will' and 'I'm keeping an eye on you
GLASSHOLE-'. His response is immediate and confrontational
showing protective instinct. The interruption breaks a private moment between him and Liz.

memories.memory_text: I swung that mace hard into Wink again and again, feeling the impact all the way up my arms, and when I had him close, I lifted him over my head and threw him off the cliff—watching him smash into the rocks below.
grounded_facts: Hellboy gains upper hand in fight with Wink
Hellboy uses Wink's chain and mace against him
Hellboy lifts Wink overhead and throws him off cliff
Wink hits jagged rocks multiple times

memories.memory_text: After the fight, I felt the warm blood seeping from my side, pressed my hand to the bandage to check it, and said 'Ouch- ouch- ouch-' under my breath before telling Abe to keep quiet and move on.
grounded_facts: Hellboy's side wound reopens after fight
Hellboy discreetly touches bleeding side
Hellboy says 'Ouch- ouch- ouch-'
Hellboy tells Abe not to speak and to keep moving

memories.memory_text: I grabbed that rusted wheel underwater and forced it open, felt the bandage tear free and blood clouding around me, but I didn’t stop until it gave.
grounded_facts: Hellboy physically turned the underwater iron lock wheel
His bandage came off during the effort
His wound reopened
releasing blood into the water
He surfaced immediately afterward due to lack of air

memories.memory_text: I leaned against the wall on those damn stairs, wiped at my chest, and pushed past them when Liz said to rest—told her I was fine even though every step burned.
grounded_facts: Hellboy paused on the stairs and leaned against the wall
He used a rag to dab his chest wound
He verbally insisted he was fine when Liz suggested resting
He moved ahead of the group despite visible pain

memories.memory_text: I tapped the chest of one of those golden statues just to hear the echo, and when it rocked back, I knew I’d messed up—then the whole damn line started crashing down like dominoes.
grounded_facts: Hellboy physically interacted with a golden soldier by tapping its chest
The soldier rocked backward after being struck
This initiated a chain reaction causing multiple soldiers to fall in sequence

memories.memory_text: After the noise of the falling soldiers died down, another wave came from a different direction—Liz screamed, I yanked her out of the way just in time, and then I saw him: the Prince, standing there like he was waiting for us.
grounded_facts: A second wave of falling soldiers approached from another direction after the first wave ended
Liz screamed in reaction to the danger
Hellboy pulled Liz aside to protect her
The Prince was revealed at the end of the collapse sequence

memories.memory_text: I took off my pack and handed it over, and when Manning started in, I just said, 'Looks that way, doesn't it?'—then grabbed the Samaritan back before walking off with Liz and Abe.
grounded_facts: Hellboy removed his gear in front of Manning and other agents
Hellboy said 'Looks that way, doesn't it?' in response to Manning questioning his resignation
Hellboy initially left his gun behind but then retrieved it
Hellboy kept the Samaritan, indicating a personal boundary

memories.memory_text: Liz said, 'Our baby, that's who!' and suddenly everything clicked—her smile, the way she breathed deep—and then I was grinning so hard it felt like my face might split.
grounded_facts: Liz tells Hellboy they are going to have a baby.
Hellboy initially does not understand what she means by 'grow'.
Hellboy responds with a massive, visible grin after processing the news.
